Code Coverage

Source: vignettes/code-coverage.Rmd
code-coverage.Rmd

Purpose

The code-coverage workflow measures unit test coverage for the package and generates coverage reports and badges.

Inputs

  • r-version: The version of R to use. Default: 'release'. See setup-r action inputs.
  • skip-coverage-badges: Skip code coverage badge creation. Default: false.

Jobs

coverage Job

Runs code coverage tests, generates coverage reports, and outputs the coverage percentage.

Conditions

Executed unless the latest commit message contains [skip coverage].

Steps

  1. Set up R environment using setup_R action, installing covr.
  2. Run coverage analysis with covr:
    • Generate coverage.xml, coverage.txt, and coverage-report.html.
  3. Check for existence of coverage reports.
  4. Set coverage percentage as output.
  5. If coverage reports exist and event is a pull request:
    1. Generate coverage summary report and badge using CodeCoverageSummary.
    2. Upload coverage report artifact. A link to download the artifact is provided in the log.
    3. Add coverage summary as a pull request comment.

badge Job

Generates a code coverage badge and commits it to the badges branch.

Conditions

Runs if skip-coverage-badges is not true, commit message does not contain [skip coverage], and branch is main.

Steps

  1. Checkout the badges branch.
  2. Generate badge SVG using badge-action.
  3. Commit badge to the branch.
  4. Push badge updates.

Triggers

  • workflow_dispatch: Manual trigger, allows specifying inputs.
  • workflow_call: Triggered by another workflow, allows specifying inputs.

Requirements

To enable badge creation, create an orphan badges branch in your repository:

git checkout --orphan badges
# Remove everything (except .git)
git rm -rf .
# Create a README file
echo "# Orphan branch for badges" > README.md
# Add, commit and push your new branch
git add README.md
git commit -m "Init badges"
git push origin badges
